title: Inventory MICS ECDI, preschool-access, parenting-program, and nutrition-outcome data sources for LMICs
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates MICS ECDI survey data, preschool-enrollment data from EMIS, parenting-program evaluations, and nutrition-outcome data (DHS, MICS) into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit ECD-measurement comparability and sub-national data availability assessment.
inputs: MICS ECDI documentation and instrument-comparability studies
DHS and MICS child nutrition and development modules
National ECD program documentation for target countries
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic grain, and documented limitations — particularly ECDI instrument comparability and sub-national sample sizes.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering ECD-measurement comparability, sub-national data availability, and intervention-coverage gaps.

title: Map sub-national ECD outcomes using MICS ECDI data for at least two LMICs
outcome: A sub-national ECD-outcome analysis for at least two LMICs using MICS ECDI data, documenting instrument comparability, sub-national sample sufficiency, and parent-reporting limitations.
inputs: MICS ECDI microdata for target countries
Administrative boundary data for district-level aggregation
expected_artifact: validation.md update with ECD-outcome methodology and measurement-limitation documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms MICS data extraction, instrument-comparability assessment, and sub-national sample-sufficiency documentation for each target country.
done_condition: ECD-outcome analysis includes methodology, instrument-comparability assessment, sub-national results, and replication status for at least two countries.

title: Analyze ECD intervention-coverage gaps linking preschool access, parenting programs, and nutrition interventions to developmental outcomes
outcome: An intervention-coverage analysis linking ECD outcomes to preschool access, parenting-program availability, and nutrition-intervention coverage, with explicit coverage-vs-impact distinction and quality-variation documentation.
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with coverage methodology and coverage-vs-impact documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms coverage-linkage methodology, quality-variation documentation, and coverage-vs-impact distinction for each target country.
done_condition: Intervention-coverage analysis includes methodology, coverage-vs-impact distinction, quality-variation documentation, and replication status.

title: Red-team ECD-measurement overstatement and coverage-implies-impact risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how ECDI instrument variation could undermine cross-country comparison, how coverage data could imply impact without quality evidence, and how parenting-intervention trial results could be overgeneralized to program scale.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every ECD-outcome claim has instrument-comparability documentation, every coverage claim has quality-variation context, and every effectiveness claim has scale-transferability caveats. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every ECD and coverage claim is either blocked, softened to measurement-limited language with explicit instrument and quality caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match ECD ministry and program-implementer decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the ECD-outcome and coverage analysis would change any real decision for ECD ministries, program implementers, or funders without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for ECD ministry and implementer audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that coverage data implies impact without quality evidence, or that trial results are overgeneralized to program scale. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., UNICEF Ethiopia ECD program officer), named decision (e.g., preschool expansion targeting), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for claiming developmental impact without quality data), and replicated field-reality review.
